kept.text: $INMD out with another crappy report.  Its still cheap and has a boatload of cash, which is why I'm still long.

quoted_tweet.text:
$INMD Q1 2026 earnings: Top-Line Reverses to Growth, But Margins Compress

InMode finally broke a streak of sequential year-over-year revenue declines, posting a 5% top-line increase to $82.0 million in Q1 2026. However, the victory was heavily overshadowed by severe margin compression. Non-GAAP operating margin decelerated sharply from 23% a year ago to 17%, driving a 26% drop in non-GAAP Net Income. Management blamed the contraction on higher cost of goods, recent North American sales restructuring, and subsidiary establishments. Adding to the unease, the sudden resignation of CFO Yair Malca and Board member Dr. Michael Anghel introduces executive instability. With FY26 guidance projecting flat revenue and further margin deterioration, the company is paying a steep price to maintain its market footprint.

Full article with charts - link in bio

🐂 𝐁𝐮𝐥𝐥 𝐂𝐚𝐬𝐞
• 𝐔.𝐒. 𝐄𝐪𝐮𝐢𝐩𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐒𝐚𝐥𝐞𝐬 𝐑𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐬𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐔𝐩𝐰𝐚𝐫𝐝 — U. S. Capital Equipment revenue jumped 14% YoY to $33.7M, signaling that patient demand and clinic capital expenditures may be thawing in the company's most important geography.
• 𝐌𝐚𝐬𝐬𝐢𝐯𝐞 𝐂𝐚𝐬𝐡 𝐏𝐨𝐬𝐢𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 — The company maintains a fortress balance sheet with $537.2M in cash and short-term investments, enabling it to aggressively repurchase shares ($34.8M in Q1 alone) while it explores strategic alternatives.

🐻 𝐁𝐞𝐚𝐫 𝐂𝐚𝐬𝐞
• 𝐏𝐫𝐨𝐟𝐢𝐭𝐚𝐛𝐢𝐥𝐢𝐭𝐲 𝐃𝐞𝐜𝐞𝐥𝐞𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐑𝐚𝐩𝐢𝐝𝐥𝐲 — Despite higher sales, Non-GAAP Gross Margin fell 400 basis points YoY to 75%. The shift toward sourcing lower-margin third-party lasers and persistent tariff impacts are structurally impairing the company's historical 80%+ gross margin profile.
• 𝐄𝐱𝐞𝐜𝐮𝐭𝐢𝐯𝐞 𝐅𝐥𝐢𝐠𝐡𝐭 𝐀𝐦𝐢𝐝 𝐒𝐭𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐞𝐠𝐢𝐜 𝐑𝐞𝐯𝐢𝐞𝐰 — Losing the CFO and a key Board member is a glaring red flag, especially considering the board recently retained Bank of America to explore strategic alternatives following an unsolicited takeover bid.

⚖️ 𝐕𝐞𝐫𝐝𝐢𝐜𝐭: 🔴
Bearish. A 5% revenue beat is meaningless if it costs 400-600 basis points of margin to achieve. The structural shift to lower-margin products and executive turnover point to deeper operational struggles ahead.

𝐊𝐞𝐲 𝐓𝐡𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐬

🔴 𝐎𝐩𝐞𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐋𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐚𝐠𝐞 𝐢𝐬 𝐑𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐬𝐢𝐧𝐠 [NEW]
InMode historically operated with exceptional efficiency, but 26Q1 marks a stark break in trend. GAAP operating expenses surged 13.6% YoY to $51.5M, vastly outpacing the 5% revenue growth. Sales & marketing climbed, and general & administrative expenses nearly doubled to $5.0M. Management attributed this to North American sales team restructuring and new global subsidiaries. As a result, non-GAAP operating margin plummeted from 23% to 17%.

🟢 𝐂𝐨𝐧𝐬𝐮𝐦𝐞𝐫 𝐓𝐫𝐚𝐝𝐞-𝐃𝐨𝐰𝐧 𝐀𝐜𝐜𝐞𝐥𝐞𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐍𝐨𝐧-𝐈𝐧𝐯𝐚𝐬𝐢𝐯𝐞 𝐌𝐢𝐱
The macroeconomic backdrop—defined by high interest rates and consumer caution—is causing a rapid mix shift. Non-invasive technology revenue surged, representing 22% of total revenue in 26Q1, up dramatically from 10% a year ago. Minimally invasive treatments, which cost thousands of dollars, decelerated to 77% of the mix (from 87%). While this pressures average selling prices, it proves InMode can capture demand from budget-conscious patients.

🔴 𝐂𝐅𝐎 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐁𝐨𝐚𝐫𝐝 𝐑𝐞𝐬𝐢𝐠𝐧𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐬 𝐒𝐩𝐚𝐫𝐤 𝐔𝐧𝐜𝐞𝐫𝐭𝐚𝐢𝐧𝐭𝐲 [NEW]
The abrupt, immediate-effect resignations of CFO Yair Malca and Board member Dr. Michael Anghel introduce substantial risk. While standard "no disagreement" clauses were filed, losing top financial leadership is concerning, especially as the company is currently engaged with Bank of America to explore strategic alternatives. A transition period is in place, but this vacuum limits management's ability to execute a turnaround.

🟢 𝐂𝐨𝐧𝐬𝐮𝐦𝐚𝐛𝐥𝐞𝐬 𝐁𝐚𝐬𝐞 𝐑𝐞𝐦𝐚𝐢𝐧𝐬 𝐒𝐭𝐚𝐛𝐥𝐞
Consumables and service revenue grew 6% YoY to $21.4M. This recurring revenue stream indicates that despite cautious consumer behavior and GLP-1 drug adoption (which management previously flagged as a headwind for fat-reduction procedures), InMode's installed base of physicians continues to perform treatments at a steady clip.

🟢 𝐈𝐧𝐧𝐨𝐯𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐅𝐨𝐜𝐮𝐬: 𝐏𝐢𝐜𝐨 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐄𝐫𝐛𝐢𝐮𝐦 𝐋𝐚𝐬𝐞𝐫𝐬
Management continues to execute its strategy of releasing two new platforms per year. The integration of the new Korean-sourced Pico laser and the proprietary Morpheus/Erbium YAG combination expands the Total Addressable Market into the 'bread and butter' laser segment. While this third-party sourcing is actively compressing gross margins, it is a necessary driver to keep clinic upgrade cycles moving in a saturated market.

🔴 𝐀𝐠𝐠𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐬𝐢𝐯𝐞 𝐒𝐡𝐚𝐫𝐞 𝐑𝐞𝐩𝐮𝐫𝐜𝐡𝐚𝐬𝐞𝐬 𝐂𝐨𝐧𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐮𝐞
Capital allocation remains heavily skewed toward buybacks. The company repurchased 2.55 million shares at an average price of $13.65 in 26Q1, deploying $34.8 million. While this supports the stock price in the near term, it also signals a lack of compelling M&A targets despite management's previous statements about pivoting cash usage toward acquisitions.

𝐎𝐭𝐡𝐞𝐫 𝐊𝐏𝐈𝐬

𝐈𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐫𝐧𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐚𝐥 𝐂𝐚𝐩𝐢𝐭𝐚𝐥 𝐄𝐪𝐮𝐢𝐩𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐑𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐧𝐮𝐞: $27.0 million
Decelerating. International sales dipped 4% YoY from $28.1M in 25Q1. This contrasts sharply with management's prior narrative of international operations acting as the primary growth engine offseting U. S. weakness. U. S. capital equipment, conversely, grew 14% to $33.7M.

𝐍𝐞𝐭 𝐈𝐧𝐜𝐨𝐦𝐞 (𝐆𝐀𝐀𝐏): $11.6 million
Decelerating sharply. Net income fell 36% YoY from $18.2 million. This is a clear indicator that volume growth at the top line is completely detached from bottom-line profitability due to structural cost increases.

𝐆𝐮𝐢𝐝𝐚𝐧𝐜𝐞

𝐅𝐘𝟐𝟔 𝐑𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐧𝐮𝐞: $365 - $375 million
Stable. The midpoint of $370M implies completely flat growth compared to FY25 actuals ($370.5M). It indicates management does not expect the 5% YoY growth seen in Q1 to persist through the remainder of the year.

𝐅𝐘𝟐𝟔 𝐍𝐨𝐧-𝐆𝐀𝐀𝐏 𝐆𝐫𝐨𝐬𝐬 𝐌𝐚𝐫𝐠𝐢𝐧: 74% - 76%
Decelerating. This is a severe step down from FY25's 79%. The compression explicitly confirms that the shift toward lower-margin, third-party sourced lasers and ongoing tariff impacts are permanent structural headwinds, not temporary blips.

𝐅𝐘𝟐𝟔 𝐍𝐨𝐧-𝐆𝐀𝐀𝐏 𝐄𝐚𝐫𝐧𝐢𝐧𝐠𝐬 𝐏𝐞𝐫 𝐒𝐡𝐚𝐫𝐞: $1.33 - $1.38
Decelerating. The midpoint ($1.355) represents a roughly 15% decline from FY25's non-GAAP EPS of $1.60. Earnings recession is expected to deepen despite heavy share repurchases artificially supporting the per-share metrics.

𝐊𝐞𝐲 𝐐𝐮𝐞𝐬𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐬

𝐂𝐅𝐎 𝐃𝐞𝐩𝐚𝐫𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐞 𝐂𝐨𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐱𝐭
What drove the sudden departure of the CFO in the middle of a strategic alternatives review, and how does this impact the timeline of the Bank of America evaluation?

𝐌𝐚𝐫𝐠𝐢𝐧 𝐅𝐥𝐨𝐨𝐫 𝐨𝐧 𝐓𝐡𝐢𝐫𝐝-𝐏𝐚𝐫𝐭𝐲 𝐋𝐚𝐬𝐞𝐫𝐬
With non-GAAP gross margin guided down to the mid-70s, what is the ultimate margin floor as third-party sourced products like the Pico laser make up a larger portion of the revenue mix?

𝐔.𝐒. 𝐯𝐬 𝐈𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐫𝐧𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐚𝐥 𝐑𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐬𝐚𝐥
U. S. capital equipment grew 14% while International shrank 4%. Is this a sustainable reversal of the trend seen throughout 2025, or merely a timing irregularity regarding product launches?

𝐂𝐥𝐚𝐬𝐬 𝐀𝐜𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐋𝐚𝐰𝐬𝐮𝐢𝐭 𝐒𝐭𝐚𝐭𝐮𝐬
The motion to dismiss the second amended complaint is pending. How is the company planning for potential financial impacts, and are legal fees associated with this defense materially impacting G&A expenses?

profile_bio.description:
Using AI to summarise companies' earnings. Not advice. AI could make mistakes, double check the output.

Articles with charts - https://t.co/XPvKIyrLjC

kept.text:
$INMD 𝐈𝐧𝐌𝐨𝐝𝐞: 𝐑𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐧𝐮𝐞 𝐆𝐫𝐨𝐰𝐭𝐡 𝐑𝐞𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐧𝐬, 𝐁𝐮𝐭 𝐌𝐚𝐫𝐠𝐢𝐧 𝐏𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐬𝐮𝐫𝐞 𝐂𝐨𝐧𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐮𝐞𝐬

📊 𝐑𝐞𝐬𝐮𝐥𝐭𝐬
• Revenue: $82.0M (vs. $77.9M YoY) ✅
• Consumables & service revenue: $21.4M (+6% YoY)
• GAAP gross margin: 75% (vs. 78%)
• GAAP operating margin: 12% (vs. 20%)
• GAAP net income: $11.6M (vs. $18.2M)
• GAAP EPS: $0.18 (vs. $0.26)
• Non-GAAP EPS: $0.25 (vs. $0.31)
• Cash & investments: $537.2M
⠀
🎯 𝐆𝐮𝐢𝐝𝐚𝐧𝐜𝐞
• FY26 revenue: $365M–$375M
• Non-GAAP gross margin: 74%–76%
• Non-GAAP operating income: $73M–$78M
• Non-GAAP EPS: $1.33–$1.38
⠀
📌 𝐊𝐞𝐲 𝐓𝐚𝐤𝐞𝐚𝐰𝐚𝐲𝐬
• Revenue growth returned despite softer aesthetics market
• Margins pressured by higher COGS and sales team restructuring
• Company continues aggressive share repurchases
• Strong balance sheet with over $537M cash and investments
• CFO transition and board chair change announced
• Management sees deferred — not destroyed — aesthetics demand
⠀
💬 𝐌𝐚𝐧𝐚𝐠𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐂𝐨𝐦𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐚𝐫𝐲
“While the demand in the aesthetics market may be deferred, it will not be diminished, and we believe we are well positioned for its return.”

quoted_tweet.text:
Haven’t been very active this week because this market action just doesn’t make much sense to me 
 
$SPY is pushing new highs up 12%+ since the beginning of April while we’ve got wars ongoing and inflation pressures globally.  
 
That kind of disconnect isn’t something I feel confident trading aggressively in 
 
My main watch is for when big cap volume starts rotating into small caps. That’s when things can get really interesting But we’re just not there yet 
 
Small caps have mostly been making new lows with only a handful of names putting up 30 to 40%+ days  
 
the volume tells the story:  
 
May 5 2026 — 24,532,700 
May 4 2026 — 28,632,300 
May 1 2026 — 28,936,700 
Apr 30 2026 — 29,220,700 
Apr 29 2026 — 29,428,900 
Apr 28 2026 — 24,139,400 
Apr 27 2026 — 23,399,400 
 
When you start getting into that 60 to 70M range that’s when you typically see multiple runners going 100%+ 
 
Until then I’m staying patient and protecting capital The opportunity will come no need to force it
